hints: From 1 blue (2 red), a draw turns a red blue w.p. 2/3 ⇒ mean 3/2 draws.
From 2 blue (1 red), success w.p. 1/3 ⇒ mean 3 draws.
solution: Going from $1$ to $2$ blue requires drawing one of the $2$ reds (prob $\tfrac23$), a geometric mean of $\tfrac32$ draws. From $2$ to $3$ blue needs the last red (prob $\tfrac13$), mean $3$. Total $=\tfrac32+3=\tfrac92.$

Pooled variance $s_p^2=\dfrac{19(22^2)+19(20^2)}{38}=442$, so $s_p\approx21.02$ and the standard error is $21.02\sqrt{\tfrac1{20}+\tfrac1{20}}\approx6.65$. Then $t=\dfrac{78-67}{6.65}\approx1.655$ on $38$ df, giving a two-tailed $p\approx0.106.$

hints: Cats × mice-per-cat with both >1 and mice-per-cat > cats.
solution: $999919=991\times1009$, both prime. With more than one cat, each killing more than one mouse and more mice than there are cats, the only fit is $991$ cats each killing $1009$ mice ($1009>991$). So there were $991$ cats.
Reducing mod $3$, $y\equiv1$, so the minimal positive value is $b=1$. Then $3x=10000-7=9993$ gives $x=a=3331$. Hence $ab=3331.$

hints: First passage to 0 from n in m=n+2k steps uses k up and n+k down moves.
solution: Reaching $0$ for the first time in $m=n+2k$ steps requires $k$ wins and $n+k$ losses, and the ballot formula gives $f=\dfrac{n}{m}\binom{m}{k}p^k(1-p)^{n+k}$. For $n=5,k=3,p=\tfrac13$: $\dfrac5{11}\binom{11}{3}\big(\tfrac13\big)^3\big(\tfrac23\big)^8\approx0.1084.$

hints: Each step lands on a uniform node — identical to a 10-sided die (coupon collector).
solution: Each step jumps to a uniformly random node, exactly the coupon-collector setup with $10$ coupons, so the expected number of steps is $10H_{10}=10\big(1+\tfrac12+\cdots+\tfrac1{10}\big)\approx29.29\approx29.$

hints: Sticky-strike: implied vol is pinned to the strike, independent of spot moves.
The K=13 vol doesn't change when S moves to 15.
solution: Under a sticky-strike model the implied volatility is a function of the strike alone and does not move with the underlying. So the $K=13$ option keeps $\sigma=0.3.$

hints: Need no match Mon, no match Tue, match Wed.
Count favorable splits over all C(10,2)C(8,2)C(6,2) ordered selections.
solution: Requiring mismatched draws Monday and Tuesday and a matched draw Wednesday, counting favorable selections over all $\binom{10}{2}\binom82\binom62$ ordered triples gives $P=\dfrac{26}{315}.$

hints: Two chords with 4 distinct endpoints cross w.p. 1/3; adjacent segments share a vertex and can't cross interior.
solution: Two segments on distinct endpoints intersect inside with probability $\tfrac13$; the $n$ adjacent pairs share a vertex and don't. With $\binom n2-n$ eligible pairs, $\mathbb{E}=\dfrac{\binom n2-n}{3}$. For $n=12$: $\dfrac{66-12}{3}=18.$

hints: Statement n ('at most n−1 true') is true iff the true-count T ≤ n−1.
So exactly the statements n=T+1..100 are true: 100−T of them.
solution: Statement $n$ is true exactly when the number of true statements $T$ satisfies $T\le n-1$, i.e. for $n\ge T+1$. That makes $100-T$ statements true, so $100-T=T$ and $T=50.$
